title: Dasylepis leonensis
intro: Dasylepis leonensis, also known as the West African Dasylepis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine, where its leaves and roots are valued for their health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and various skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Dasylepis leonensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of a poultice or ointment to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Dasylepis leonensis is a species of plant in the family Achariaceae, within the order Oxalidales and class Magnoliopsida. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a flowering plant. The species is also referred to as a member of the genus Dasylepis. The classification further places it in the division Magnoliophyta. This classification makes Dasylepis leonensis a part of the broader plant kingdom.
